devanagari: शाल्वस्य यदुभिः सह युद्धम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । अथान्यदपि कृष्णस्य श्रृणु कर्माद्भुतं नृप । क्रीडानरशरीरस्य यथा सौभपतिर्हतः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śālvasya yadubhiḥ saha yuddham śrīśuka uvāca athānyadapi kṛṣṇasya śrṛṇu karmādbhutaṃ nṛpa krīḍānaraśarīrasya yathā saubhapatirhataḥ

translation: He has now returned to his country which is militarily fully prepared with army and transport (horses, chariots, etc). Out of (all) princes, he is the only king actively preparing (for war), O king.

devanagari: शिशुपालसखः शाल्वो रुक्मिण्युद्वाह आगतः । यदुभिर्निर्जितः सङ्ख्ये जरासन्धादयस्तथा ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śiśupālasakhaḥ śālvo rukmiṇyudvāha āgataḥ yadubhirnirjitaḥ saṅkhye jarāsandhādayastathā
translation: Having heard this report as detailed by Nārada, the affluent king Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma, quietly looked at the face of the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa who was seated by his side.

devanagari: शाल्वः प्रतिज्ञामकरोत् श्रृण्वतां सर्वभूभुजाम् । अयादवां क्ष्मां करिष्ये पौरुषं मम पश्यत ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śālvaḥ pratijñāmakarot śrṛṇvatāṃ sarvabhūbhujām ayādavāṃ kṣmāṃ kariṣye pauruṣaṃ mama paśyata
translation: Listening to that report, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the essence of courage and fortitude, was not disturbed at all. Laughing a bit loudly he remarked, “So be it”, That is the index of his greatness.

devanagari: इति मूढः प्रतिज्ञाय देवं पशुपतिं प्रभुम् । आराधयामास नृपः पांसुमुष्टिं सकृद् ग्रसन् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti mūḍhaḥ pratijñāya devaṃ paśupatiṃ prabhum ārādhayāmāsa nṛpaḥ pāṃsumuṣṭiṃ sakṛd grasan

translation: Having worshipped the sage and paying him respects with sweet words, he thought, “It is a good thing that he who was wanted and expected for a long time, has also come now.”

devanagari: संवत्सरान्ते भगवान् आशुतोष उमापतिः । वरेण च्छन्दयामास शाल्वं शरणमागतम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃvatsarānte bhagavān āśutoṣa umāpatiḥ vareṇa cchandayāmāsa śālvaṃ śaraṇamāgatam
translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Lakṣmī, immediately despatched Ugrasena, King of Yādavas. Yuyudhāna, Akrūra, Kṛtavarmā and other prominent Yādava warriors along with his contingent of army to defend Dvārakā (against the expected attack from Śālva.)

devanagari: देवासुरमनुष्याणां गन्धर्वोरगरक्षसाम् । अभेद्यं कामगं वव्रे स यानं वृष्णिभीषणम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: devāsuramanuṣyāṇāṃ gandharvoragarakṣasām abhedyaṃ kāmagaṃ vavre sa yānaṃ vṛṣṇibhīṣaṇam

translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Lakṣmī, immediately despatched Ugrasena, King of Yādavas. Yuyudhāna, Akrūra, Kṛtavarmā and other prominent Yādava warriors along with his contingent of army to defend Dvārakā (against the expected attack from Śālva.)

devanagari: तथेति गिरिशादिष्टो मयः परपुरंजयः । पुरं निर्माय शाल्वाय प्रादात् सौभमयस्मयम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti giriśādiṣṭo mayaḥ parapuraṃjayaḥ puraṃ nirmāya śālvāya prādāt saubhamayasmayam

translation:
Śrī Suka continued:
While the noble-souled Govinda was thus staying at Indra- Prastha and king Yudhiṣṭhira along with his younger brothers had accomplished his purpose (the completion of the Rājasūya sacrifice), Śālva heard that his friend Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa and Jarāsandha had been killed and the Rājasūya had been completed and he got enraged.

devanagari: स लब्ध्वा कामगं यानं तमोधाम दुरासदम् । ययस्द्वारवतीं शाल्वो वैरं वृष्णिकृतं स्मरन् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa labdhvā kāmagaṃ yānaṃ tamodhāma durāsadam yayasdvāravatīṃ śālvo vairaṃ vṛṣṇikṛtaṃ smaran

translation:
Śrī Suka continued:
While the noble-souled Govinda was thus staying at Indra- Prastha and king Yudhiṣṭhira along with his younger brothers had accomplished his purpose (the completion of the Rājasūya sacrifice), Śālva heard that his friend Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa and Jarāsandha had been killed and the Rājasūya had been completed and he got enraged.

devanagari: निरुध्य सेनया शाल्वो महत्या भरतर्षभ । पुरीं बभञ्जोपवनान् उद्यानानि च सर्वशः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nirudhya senayā śālvo mahatyā bharatarṣabha purīṃ babhañjopavanān udyānāni ca sarvaśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Śālva laid siege to Dvārakā with a mighty army, O prominent Bharata. He razed to ground city parks, gardens, completely.

devanagari: सगोपुराणि द्वाराणि प्रासादाट्टालतोलिकाः । विहारान् स विमानाग्र्यान् निपेतुः शस्त्रवृष्टयः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sagopurāṇi dvārāṇi prāsādāṭṭālatolikāḥ vihārān sa vimānāgryān nipetuḥ śastravṛṣṭayaḥ
translation: He destroyed the city along with its towers, gates, mansions, balconies, terraces and places of recreation. Destructive weapons were showered from that formidable aerial car (a veritable floating city).

devanagari: शिला द्रुमाश्चाशनयः सर्पा आसारशर्कराः । प्रचण्डश्चक्रवातोऽभूत् रजसाच्छादिता दिशः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śilā drumāścāśanayaḥ sarpā āsāraśarkarāḥ pracaṇḍaścakravāto

translation: Heavily fell down (upon Dvārakā) huge stones, trees, thunderbolts, serpents, a shower of gravel (hail-stones?). Terrific tornadoes swept (across the city); the cardinal points were darkened with heavy dust.

devanagari: इत्यर्द्यमाना सौभेन कृष्णस्य नगरी भृशम् । नाभ्यपद्यत शं राजन् त्रिपुरेण यथा मही ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: ityardyamānā saubhena kṛṣṇasya nagarī bhṛśam nābhyapadyata śaṃ rājan tripureṇa yathā mahī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Just as the earth was (formerly) oppressed under the scourage of Tripura, the city of Kṛṣṇa was subjected to extreme harassment by Saubha and could not get any respite or comfort.

devanagari: प्रद्युम्नो भगवान् वीक्ष्य बाध्यमाना निजाः प्रजाः । म भैष्टेत्यभ्यधाद् वीरो रथारूढो महायशाः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: pradyumno bhagavān vīkṣya bādhyamānā nijāḥ prajāḥ ma bhaiṣṭetyabhyadhād vīro rathārūḍho mahāyaśāḥ

translation: The glorious Pradyumna noticed how his people were subjected to distress. Encouraging them with words “Don’t fear”, the highly illustrious warrior mounted his chariot.

devanagari: सात्यकिश्चारुदेष्णश्च साम्बोऽक्रूरः सहानुजः । हार्दिक्यो भानुविन्दश्च गदश्च शुकसारणौ ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sātyakiścārudeṣṇaśca sāmbo hārdikyo bhānuvindaśca gadaśca śukasāraṇau
translation: Sātyaki, Cārudeṣṇa, Sāmba, Akrūra and his younger brothers, Kṛtavarmā, Bhānuvinda, Gada, Śuka and Sāraṇa and other renowned leaders of the army followed him in their chariots equipped with bows and arms and accoutred in coats of mail and protected by chariots, elephants, cavalry and foot-soldiers.

devanagari: अपरे च महेष्वासा रथयूथपयूथपाः । निर्ययुर्दंशिता गुप्ता रथेभाश्वपदातिभिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: apare ca maheṣvāsā rathayūthapayūthapāḥ niryayurdaṃśitā guptā rathebhāśvapadātibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ gup class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ gup class 4 verb]

translation: Sātyaki, Cārudeṣṇa, Sāmba, Akrūra and his younger brothers, Kṛtavarmā, Bhānuvinda, Gada, Śuka and Sāraṇa and other renowned leaders of the army followed him in their chariots equipped with bows and arms and accoutred in coats of mail and protected by chariots, elephants, cavalry and foot-soldiers.

devanagari: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं शाल्वानां यदुभिः सह । यथासुराणां विबुधैः तुमुलं लोमहर्षणम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṃ śālvānāṃ yadubhiḥ saha yathāsurāṇāṃ vibudhaiḥ tumulaṃ lomaharṣaṇam

translation: Then there raged a battle between the army of Śālva and that of the Yādavas, tumultuous and horripulating like the (bitter) struggle between gods and Asuras (in ancient times).

devanagari: ताश्च सौभपतेर्माया दिव्यास्त्रै रुक्मिणीसुतः । क्षणेन नाशयामास नैशं तम इवोष्णगुः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tāśca saubhapatermāyā divyāstrai rukmiṇīsutaḥ kṣaṇena nāśayāmāsa naiśaṃ tama ivoṣṇaguḥ
translation: Just as the sun (the hot-rayed, luminary) dispells the darkness of the night instantaneously, Pradyumna, the son of Rukmiṇī destroyed in a moment all the black magical spells of Śālva, the master of Saubha, with his celestial missiles.

devanagari: विव्याध पञ्चविंशत्या स्वर्णपुङ्खैरयोमुखैः । शाल्वस्य ध्वजिनीपालं शरैः सन्नतपर्वभिः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: vivyādha pañcaviṃśatyā svarṇapuṅkhairayomukhaiḥ śālvasya dhvajinīpālaṃ śaraiḥ sannataparvabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He pierced the commander-in-chief of Śālva’s army with twenty shafts with gold feathers, iron tips and well-polished knots (known as sannataparva)

devanagari: शतेनाताडयच्छाल्वं एकैकेनास्य सैनिकान् । दशभिर्दशभिर्नेतॄन् वाहनानि त्रिभिस्त्रिभिः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śatenātāḍayacchālvaṃ ekaikenāsya sainikān daśabhirdaśabhirnetṝn vāhanāni tribhistribhiḥ
translation: He struck Śālva with a hundred arrows and every one of his soldiers with one shaft each, leaders of every battalion (or charioteers) with ten arrows and the horses with three each.

devanagari: तदद्भुतं महत् कर्म प्रद्युम्नस्य महात्मनः । दृष्ट्वा तं पूजयामासुः सर्वे स्वपरसैनिकाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tadadbhutaṃ mahat karma pradyumnasya mahātmanaḥ dṛṣṭvā taṃ pūjayāmāsuḥ sarve svaparasainikāḥ
translation: Observing that great and wonderful feat of the high- souled Pradymna, soldiers on both the sides applauded him.

devanagari: बहुरूपैकरूपं तद् दृश्यते न च दृश्यते । मायामयं मयकृतं दुर्विभाव्यं परैरभूत् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: bahurūpaikarūpaṃ tad dṛśyate na ca dṛśyate māyāmayaṃ mayakṛtaṃ durvibhāvyaṃ parairabhūt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: But the aerial-car Saubha constructed by Maya had such a magical structure and contrivances that sometimes it appeared many, sometimes one, and at times it was invisible. It was thus very difficult for the enemies (Yādavas) to locate it or conceive its nature and whereabouts.

devanagari: क्वचिद्भूमौ क्वचिद् व्योम्नि गिरिमूर्ध्नि जले क्वचित् । अलातचक्रवद् भ्राम्यत् सौभं तद् दुरवस्थितम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacidbhūmau kvacid vyomni girimūrdhni jale kvacit alātacakravad bhrāmyat saubhaṃ tad duravasthitam
translation: Sometimes Saubha was on the ground; sometimes it flew into the sky; at times it rested on the summit of a mountain; sometimes it floated on water; sometimes it whirled round like a fire-brand and was not stationary at any point—(and thus was impossible to locate).

devanagari: यत्र यत्रोपलक्ष्येत ससौभः सहसैनिकः । शाल्वः ततस्ततोऽमुञ्चन् शरान् सात्वतयूथपाः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra yatropalakṣyeta sasaubhaḥ sahasainikaḥ śālvaḥ tatastato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: So the leaders of the Yādava army discharged arrows at every such point where Śālva with his aerial car Saubha and his soldiers made their appearance.

devanagari: शरैरग्न्यर्कसंस्पर्शैः आशीविषदुरासदैः । पीड्यमानपुरानीकः शाल्वोऽमुह्यत् परेरितैः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: śarairagnyarkasaṃsparśaiḥ āśīviṣadurāsadaiḥ pīḍyamānapurānīkaḥ śālvo
translation: With his floating city Saubha and his army being sorely oppressed by the arrows discharged by the Yādava enemies—arrows the touch of which was scorching like the fire and the sun and unapproachable like venomous serpents Śālva fainted.

devanagari: शाल्वानीकपशस्त्रौघैः वृष्णिवीरा भृशार्दिताः । न तत्यजू रणं स्वं स्वं लोकद्वयजिगीषवः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śālvānīkapaśastraughaiḥ vṛṣṇivīrā bhṛśārditāḥ na tatyajū raṇaṃ svaṃ svaṃ lokadvayajigīṣavaḥ
translation: Though the Yādava warriors were severely beaten by the volleys of weapons discharged by the leaders of Śālva’s army, none of them deserted the battlefield or his respective (appointed) position, for they wanted to win both the worlds (viz. their sovereignty in the world if survived or heavenly happiness if killed) in the battle.

devanagari: शाल्वामात्यो द्युमान् नाम प्रद्युम्नं प्राक् प्रपीडितः । आसाद्य गदया मौर्व्या व्याहत्य व्यनदद् बली ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śālvāmātyo dyumān nāma pradyumnaṃ prāk prapīḍitaḥ āsādya gadayā maurvyā vyāhatya vyanadad balī
translation: A minister of Śālva called Dyuman, a mighty warrior himself but who was previously heavily beaten (by Pradyumna), made a surprise attack on Pradyumna and striking him with a heavy iron mace, roared loudly.

devanagari: प्रद्युम्नं गदया शीर्ण वक्षःस्थलमरिंदमम् । अपोवाह रणात् सूतो धर्मविद् दारुकात्मजः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: pradyumnaṃ gadayā śīrṇa vakṣaḥsthalamariṃdamam apovāha raṇāt sūto dharmavid dārukātmajaḥ

translation: Pradyumna’s charioteer, the son of Dāruka (Kṛṣṇa’s charioteer) who knew his duty (as a charioteer), carried away from the battlefield Pradyumna, the chastiser of enemies whose chest was gravely wounded by the stroke of the mace.

devanagari: लब्धसंज्ञो मुहूर्तेन कार्ष्णिः सारथिमब्रवीत् । अहो असाध्विदं सूत यद् रणान्मेऽपसर्पणम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhasaṃjño muhūrtena kārṣṇiḥ sārathimabravīt aho asādhvidaṃ sūta yad raṇānme

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]

translation: When Pradyumna, the son of Kṛṣṇa regained his consciousness after some time he blamed his charioteer, “Alas! you have not done well in that you have removed me from the battle-field.

devanagari: न यदूनां कुले जातः श्रूयते रणविच्युतः । विना मत्क्लीबचित्तेन सूतेन प्राप्तकिल्बिषात् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: na yadūnāṃ kule jātaḥ śrūyate raṇavicyutaḥ vinā matklībacittena sūtena prāptakilbiṣāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]
[instrumental single from √ su class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ su class 2 verb]
[instrumental single from √ su class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ su class 2 verb]

translation: No person born in Yadu’s race is known to have turned away from the battle-field except me who incurred the blemish through a weak-minded charioteer like you.

devanagari: किं नु वक्ष्येऽभिसङ्गम्य पितरौ रामकेशवौ । युद्धात् सम्यगपक्रान्तः पृष्टस्तत्रात्मनः क्षमम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ nu vakṣye yuddhāt samyagapakrāntaḥ pṛṣṭastatrātmanaḥ kṣamam

translation: Having deliberately and obviously left the battlefield, what should I plead to my uncle Balarāma and father Kṛṣṇa when they meet me and enquire about my ability.

devanagari: व्यक्तं मे कथयिष्यन्ति हसन्त्यो भ्रातृजामयः । क्लैब्यं कथं कथं वीर तवान्यैः कथ्यतां मृधे ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vyaktaṃ me kathayiṣyanti hasantyo bhrātṛjāmayaḥ klaibyaṃ kathaṃ kathaṃ vīra tavānyaiḥ kathyatāṃ mṛdhe

translation: My brothers and sisters-in-law will jeer at me and openly ask me, “How did you fare with your enemies in the battle? How is it that you played the coward?”

devanagari: सारथिरुवाच । धर्मं विजानताऽयुष्मन् कृतमेतन्मया विभो । सूतः कृच्छ्रगतं रक्षेद् रथिनं सारथिं रथी ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sārathiruvāca dharmaṃ vijānatā sūtaḥ kṛcchragataṃ rakṣed rathinaṃ sārathiṃ rathī

translation:
The charioteer replied:
(It is) With full knowledge of my duty as a charioteer that I have done this, O long-lived one. My Lord, the prescribed duty is that the charioteer should protect the warrior in times of peril and the warrior should reciprocally protect him.

devanagari: एतद् विदित्वा तु भवान् मयापोवाहितो रणात् । उपसृष्टः परेणेति मूर्च्छितो गदया हतः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: etad viditvā tu bhavān mayāpovāhito raṇāt upasṛṣṭaḥ pareṇeti mūrcchito gadayā hataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Knowing this duty as a charioteer, I took you away from the battlefield as, when attacked by the enemy and struck down with a mace, you became unconscions in a Swoon.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । शाल्वयुद्धे षट्सप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७६ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe śālvayuddhe ṣaṭsaptatitamo
